jokes.text: Hey, just let him know that the few times that I have been naughty, it was real nice.
jokes.setup: Will asking Granny to put in a good word with Santa
jokes.punchline: Turning 'naughty or nice' into sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Classic Will Smith character move - taking innocent situation and adding sexual subtext with perfect delivery and timing

jokes.text: And if I said I weren't, would you leave me alone?
jokes.setup: Will enthusiastically greeting Geoffrey about Christmas cookies
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's dry, sarcastic deflection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey character moment - his perpetual annoyance delivered with British wit

jokes.text: Today is the happiest day of my life. Until tomorrow, when my show goes national... which will make that the new happiest day of my life. That'll make today...
jokes.setup: Hilary describing today as the happiest day
jokes.punchline: Getting confused by her own logic about which day is happiest
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Hilary's airheaded nature - getting lost in her own circular reasoning

jokes.text: I get the pattern, Miss Hilary.
jokes.setup: Hilary's confusing explanation about which day will be happiest
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey cutting through her nonsense with dry observation
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey as perfect straight man, deflating Hilary's rambling with minimal words

jokes.text: Click. Heh, heh. Look, I'm on.
jokes.setup: Hilary practicing how Uncle Phil will watch her show
jokes.punchline: Making clicking sound and pretending to be on TV
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag showing Hilary's self-absorption and TV obsession

jokes.text: A bunch of pregnant women. - Great, I'll look thin. Not that I don't, anyway.
jokes.setup: Will telling Hilary her guests are pregnant women
jokes.punchline: Hilary immediately thinking about how it makes her look, then insecure backtrack
jokes.explanation: Classic Hilary vanity moment with perfect character-specific self-consciousness

jokes.text: But beware, in that last trimester, stripes are not our friends.
jokes.setup: Hilary giving maternity fashion advice on her show
jokes.punchline: Treating pregnancy fashion like high fashion with dramatic warning
jokes.explanation: Hilary treating mundane topic with fashion world seriousness - very character appropriate

jokes.text: Werner, if you don't stop dancing, I'm about to get seasick.
jokes.setup: Werner dancing enthusiastically to celebrate
jokes.punchline: Phil's motion sickness from watching the dancing
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy reaction - functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: They like me. They really like me.
jokes.setup: Reporter asking how Hilary feels about success
jokes.punchline: Parodying Sally Field's famous Oscar speech
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference that works for character but is fairly obvious and overused

jokes.text: My water broke.
jokes.setup: Hilary at peak of success, being interviewed
jokes.punchline: Pregnant guest's water breaking during live TV
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and escalation - turns moment of triumph into chaos with economy and impact

jokes.text: Now, this is just like on ER, y'all. All right, so just chill. Everybody, just relax.
jokes.setup: Medical emergency happening
jokes.punchline: Will thinking TV medical shows qualify him to help
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's overconfidence and pop culture obsession

jokes.text: Can somebody get this clown out of here?
jokes.setup: Will trying to help with medical emergency
jokes.punchline: Real doctor dismissing Will as incompetent clown
jokes.explanation: Straightforward comeuppance for Will's overconfidence - functional but predictable

jokes.text: Whoa. What a coincidence, Hil, this is the happiest day of your life too. Your show just went national. - I know. Don't I look happy?
jokes.setup: Will pointing out this should be Hilary's happiest day
jokes.punchline: Hilary's deadpan response while clearly stressed/unhappy
jokes.explanation: Nice ironic callback showing how the 'happiest day' became a disaster

jokes.text: Hey, you know what? Right after you wash that.
jokes.setup: Doctor wants to shake hands after delivering baby
jokes.punchline: Will realizing what the doctor's hands have been doing
jokes.explanation: Predictable gross-out humor but delivered with Will's characteristic awareness

jokes.text: Hey, you know, it's the perfect size for, like, I don't know... a THX, Dolby Pro Logic, surround-sound subwoofer.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil commenting on baby's size
jokes.punchline: Will immediately thinking about audio equipment instead of baby
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will moment - completely missing emotional point for material obsession

jokes.text: It makes it look cheesy... and I won't have it on my tree. - Oh, your tree?
jokes.setup: Ashley and Carlton decorating tree together
jokes.punchline: Ownership dispute escalating from cooperation to conflict
jokes.explanation: Typical sibling rivalry escalation - shows character dynamics well

jokes.text: Let a brother get a finger full.
jokes.setup: Will trying to get cookie dough from Granny
jokes.punchline: Using street slang for simple request while being physically rebuffed
jokes.explanation: Classic Will Smith delivery mixing street vernacular with innocent request

jokes.text: I just can't seem to get this bowl to stand still. - It might help if you put down the eggnog.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey complaining about bowl moving
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's drunk, which explains the 'moving' bowl
jokes.explanation: Well-crafted setup and reveal showing Geoffrey's holiday drinking, good structure

jokes.text: Sure, I have an effervescent personality... and a smile that could blind you from across the room... but I just feel so blah.
jokes.setup: Hilary describing her emptiness despite success
jokes.punchline: Listing her amazing qualities while claiming to feel 'blah'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - self-absorbed even while vulnerable, very character-specific

jokes.text: Oh, I'm sorry, were you saying something, honey? You've gotta talk into my good ear.
jokes.setup: Hilary pouring her heart out about feeling empty
jokes.punchline: Granny revealing she wasn't listening at all
jokes.explanation: Excellent comedic timing - deflates Hilary's self-important monologue perfectly

jokes.text: Phoenix, Phoenix - This is the Hilary Show Phoenix, Phoenix
jokes.setup: Werner trying to help Hilary with promo energy
jokes.punchline: Completely over-the-top, ridiculous delivery
jokes.explanation: Broad physical comedy - gets a reaction but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Something in between what you did and whatever it was that he just did.
jokes.setup: Werner's over-the-top promo demonstration
jokes.punchline: Will's diplomatic but dismissive response
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man reaction with diplomatic shade - well-delivered

jokes.text: Hil, couldn't you have said this with flowers?
jokes.setup: Will and Hilary arriving at hospital to see baby
jokes.punchline: Will suggesting flowers would be more normal than visiting
jokes.explanation: Reasonable reaction to Hilary's impulse, shows Will's practical side

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. Well, you know, bald head, pudgy. If he had a beard, he'd look just like my Uncle Phil.
jokes.setup: Being asked if he thinks the baby is cute
jokes.punchline: Comparing baby to Uncle Phil based on physical features
jokes.explanation: Classic Will roast of Uncle Phil, good visual humor and character relationship

jokes.text: Whoa. Hey, dude. Hold up. He tripping. He tripping.
jokes.setup: Hilary holding baby who starts crying
jokes.punchline: Will using street slang to describe crying baby
jokes.explanation: Will's characteristic slang applied to baby situation - good character consistency

jokes.text: Uncle Phil's hand.
jokes.setup: Looking at baby's little hands
jokes.punchline: Comparing tiny baby hand to Uncle Phil's presumably large hands
jokes.explanation: Good callback extending the previous Uncle Phil comparison with visual element

jokes.text: Oh. Remember, doc, no mouth-to-mouth on the first date, huh?
jokes.setup: Doctor offering to give them a ride
jokes.punchline: Will making mouth-to-mouth resuscitation into dating joke
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay connecting medical procedure to dating - typical Will humor

jokes.text: Oh, it's like a hotel. They expect you to take this stuff.
jokes.setup: Will apparently taking hospital supplies
jokes.punchline: Justifying theft by comparing to hotel amenities
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's opportunistic side with flawed logic

jokes.text: You know, a cow would be happy to be in your kitchen.
jokes.setup: Complimenting Grandma's cooking
jokes.punchline: Suggesting even the source animal would appreciate being cooked so well
jokes.explanation: Clever compliment with unexpected perspective - good wordplay and economy

jokes.text: Now, that would be bigger than the roast.
jokes.setup: Phil saying no news is bigger than mom's roast, then Hilary announcing pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Phil acknowledging this actually is bigger news
jokes.explanation: Good immediate callback with understated delivery - Phil's practical response

jokes.text: Geoffrey, get the Scotch. - It's gone, sir. - What? - You've said my attitude has improved lately. - How could this happen? - It's the pressure, sir.
jokes.setup: Phil needing alcohol for stress, Geoffrey revealing it's gone
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey explaining his drinking as job-related stress relief
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-driven callback with perfect setup/payoff structure

jokes.text: I think Aunt Viv must've dropped the ball on that little girl-girl talk.
jokes.setup: Hilary's confusion about pregnancy mechanics
jokes.punchline: Will suggesting inadequate sex education is the cause
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to Hilary's airheaded moment with characteristic Will observation

jokes.text: Scotch is a pick-me-up. Damn that, Geoffrey.
jokes.setup: Phil lecturing about babies not being pick-me-ups
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting himself by wanting alcohol as a pick-me-up
jokes.explanation: Good character hypocrisy moment with callback to Geoffrey drinking the Scotch

jokes.text: Hi, Grandma?
jokes.setup: Phil asking what to tell Vivian about becoming a grandmother
jokes.punchline: Will's obvious suggestion delivered questioningly
jokes.explanation: Simple obvious joke with good delivery - functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: What have you got in a hunk?
jokes.setup: Sperm bank worker asking about specific preferences
jokes.punchline: Hilary treating sperm donation like shopping for attractive men
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary line - treating scientific process like shopping, very character-specific

jokes.text: You wouldn't happen to have this month's, would you?
jokes.setup: Worker mentioning they have doctors
jokes.punchline: Hilary treating sperm bank like magazine subscription
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - perfectly captures Hilary's consumer mentality applied to reproduction

jokes.text: Ugh! I don't think so.
jokes.setup: Hilary looking through sperm bank materials
jokes.punchline: Her disgusted rejection of what she's seeing
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction shot showing Hilary's pickiness - functional but basic

jokes.text: Ugh! I don't think so.
jokes.setup: Hilary looking through donor information
jokes.punchline: Her disgusted rejection of whatever she's seeing
jokes.explanation: Hilary's shallow standards applied to sperm donors - visual comedy of her reaction

jokes.text: Hey, yo. Thanks a lot, man. - Glad I could be of service.
jokes.setup: Will apparently making a sperm donation
jokes.punchline: Casual transaction for what should be private medical procedure
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor from Will's casual attitude about intimate medical procedure

jokes.text: Oh, what'd you expect? Well, you know, after today, I kind of thought you... Oh. That was just a little setback. I'm a trouper.
jokes.setup: Will expecting Hilary to still be upset about her baby plan
jokes.punchline: Hilary dismissing her major life crisis as a 'little setback'
jokes.explanation: Hilary's ability to compartmentalize major emotional moments - very much in character

jokes.text: Well, I just hope my baby's a super-duper trouper too.
jokes.setup: Will calling Hilary a 'super-duper trouper'
jokes.punchline: Hilary extending the alliteration to her future baby
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay callback - shows Hilary still thinking about baby plan

jokes.text: Come back to deliver another baby, huh? What, you get a commission on them?
jokes.setup: Doctor arriving to pick up Hilary
jokes.punchline: Will suggesting doctors get paid per baby like salespeople
jokes.explanation: Good Will observation mixing medicine with business - characteristic humor

jokes.text: Come back to deliver another baby, huh? What, you get a commission on them?
jokes.setup: Doctor showing up again
jokes.punchline: Will suggesting doctors get paid per baby delivery like salespeople
jokes.explanation: Will's street-smart take on medical practice - treating it like any other business

jokes.text: So any mental illness in your family? Schizophrenia? Anorexia? You know, general weirdness?
jokes.setup: Hilary and doctor on a date
jokes.punchline: Hilary treating romantic dinner like medical screening
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment - Hilary's complete social obliviousness in romantic setting

jokes.text: So back to you. Any heart disease? Gingivitis? Midgets?... Any relatives in the circus?
jokes.setup: Continuing her genetic screening questions
jokes.punchline: Escalating from medical to increasingly absurd concerns ending with circus relatives
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation joke - starts medical, gets absurd. 'Midgets' and 'circus relatives' is peak Hilary shallow logic

jokes.text: Any relatives in the circus? - Uh, not that I know of. I mean... I did have an uncle who was a clown. - Ew. Duly noted.
jokes.setup: Hilary's increasingly ridiculous genetic screening questions
jokes.punchline: Doctor's innocent answer being treated as disqualifying genetic flaw
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the screening concept with perfect Hilary reaction

jokes.text: Wait, wasn't I the one asking all the questions?
jokes.setup: Doctor asking normal date questions
jokes.punchline: Hilary realizing she's supposed to be the interviewer
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Hilary's self-centered interview approach

jokes.text: Imagine that, if you ain't had no kids. It'd just be you, Aunt Viv and me all by ourselves in this big house. Party, you know.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil wondering about life without parenting headaches
jokes.punchline: Will suggesting three adults partying would be the alternative
jokes.explanation: Good Will moment showing his party mentality and missing the point

jokes.text: Hey, look, Uncle Phil, I just want you to know I got your back. All right? Anything jump off, all you got to do is holler.
jokes.setup: Tense family conversation about to happen
jokes.punchline: Will offering street-style backup for family therapy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will moment - applying street logic to domestic situation

jokes.text: You know, it used to be so easy. You'd cry, I'd buy you a bracelet. You cried louder, maybe some earrings. I miss those days.
jokes.setup: Phil reflecting on parenting getting more complicated
jokes.punchline: Nostalgic for when material gifts solved all problems
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Phil's wealthy parenting style with genuine emotion

jokes.text: Oh, please. Your side looks like someone threw up on a snowman.
jokes.setup: Carlton insulting Ashley's side of the Christmas tree
jokes.punchline: Ashley's creative and gross comeback about Carlton's side
jokes.explanation: Great visual insult with vivid imagery - good sibling rivalry moment

jokes.text: Your side? Please. I've seen better looking trees hanging from rearview mirrors.
jokes.setup: Tree decorating argument between siblings
jokes.punchline: Comparing Carlton's tree decorating to car air fresheners
jokes.explanation: Creative insult comparing bad tree decorating to car air fresheners - specific and visual

jokes.text: Yeah. I mean, you might've laid the flock on a little thick... but other than that, it's very busy.
jokes.setup: Will trying to diplomatically resolve the tree decorating fight
jokes.punchline: His backhanded compliments that actually insult both sides
jokes.explanation: Will's diplomatic failure - trying to help but making it worse with damning with faint praise

jokes.text: I'm just kind of getting the hang of this parenting thing here, know what I mean?
jokes.setup: Will successfully mediating between Ashley and Carlton
jokes.punchline: Will taking credit for parenting skills despite being the youngest
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's confidence and role reversal in the family

jokes.text: You call that dusting, Geoffrey? Give me that. I'll do it myself.
jokes.setup: Ashley asking Phil for money after parenting lecture
jokes.punchline: Phil immediately reverting to controlling behavior with Geoffrey
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency showing Phil can't help being controlling despite lessons learned

jokes.text: God bless us, everyone.
jokes.setup: Phil taking over dusting duties from Geoffrey
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's sarcastic Christmas blessing referencing Tiny Tim
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey exit line - sarcastic literary reference to cap the episode
